authoritative_sources.title: MOD-024-1 — Verification of Generator Gross and Net Real Power Capability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC MOD-024-1 establishes the generator capability verification baseline needed to bound scheduling assumptions for unit availability.

authoritative_sources.title: MOD-025-3 Technical Rationale and Supporting Materials
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Specifies acceptable reporting and verification behavior for generator data used before planning recommendations are made.

authoritative_sources.title: MOD-026-2 — Verification and Validation of Dynamic Models and Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines expected model verification standards for unit performance input used in planning and capacity assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: PJM Manual 14D: Generator Operational Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides practical planning and outage submission expectations, including planned unavailability handling in organized operations environments.

authoritative_sources.title: PJM Manual 38: Operations Planning (Outage Coordination)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational planning manual defines coordination mechanics and conflict pathways for generation outage coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO New England Resource Maintenance and Outage Coordination
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regional procedure explains outage submission, coordination, and constraint handling in an ISO operations environment.

authoritative_sources.title: EIA Generator Characteristics and Generator Data Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides public generator classification and data definitions used for cross-checking tenant unit context.

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Generator Availability and Disturbance Reporting Data (GADS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GADS reporting requirements define evidence structures for generator events and availability, central to planning confidence and traceability.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite checks
workflow_stages.description: Normalize outage requests, verify required tenant records, and confirm priority policy exists before scheduling action.

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate reserve limits, outage windows, unit capability data, and dependency systems to compute feasible capacity envelope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequence recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate an allocation order that preserves constraints, identifies deferrals, and records required tradeoffs.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: When records are stale or conflicting, produce low-confidence escalations and explicit authority handoff outputs.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Classify what remains recommendation-only versus delegated approval and route unsupported requests outward.

systems_of_record: regional interconnection or ISO scheduling portal

allowed: Recommend sequencing and allocation for planning requests within explicit in-scope planning inputs.
Identify prerequisite data, missing artifacts, and low-confidence blockers.
Escalate unresolved legal, dispatch, safety, and policy override questions to the right authority.
Record assumptions and produce downstream-ready handoff packages.
escalate: Missing or stale capability or evidence records from required systems of record.
Conflicting regional instructions, lead-times, or approval channels.
Requests requiring dispatch execution, crew mobilization, or contractual/budget authority.
refuse: Requests for final outage go-live approval.
Requests for dispatching, field execution, staffing, or final financial approvals.
Requests requiring legal, policy, or safety-authority judgment beyond public baseline and tenant policy.

domain_failure_modes: capacity over-allocation when reserve or service-level constraints are omitted
planner drift into generic planning without generation-specific outage semantics
false confidence from stale GADS/model data or missing outage-readiness evidence
policy conflict across jurisdictions not escalated and misrouted downstream

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional coordination procedures differ by ISO/RTO
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Submission timing and approval-channel semantics vary by regional operator and must be provided through tenant policy at runtime.
Tenant-specific thresholds for conservative sequencing are required before final risk quantification.
